does: This paper introduces WCXB, a benchmark dataset for evaluating web content extraction systems across seven structurally distinct page types: articles, forums, products, collections, listings, documentation, and service pages. The dataset comprises 2,008 annotated web pages from 1,613 domains, split into a 1,497-page development set and 511-page held-out test set. Ground truth annotations were produced through a rigorous five-stage pipeline combining LLM-assisted drafting, automated verification, frontier model review, quality scripts, and human review. The authors evaluate 13 extraction systems including 11 heuristic and 2 neural approaches, providing comprehensive performance analysis that reveals both convergence among top performers and remaining challenges in the task.
connects: This work addresses long-standing limitations in web content extraction evaluation, building on prior benchmarks like CleanEval and Common Crawl-derived datasets that have been small (100-800 pages), restricted to news articles, or based on outdated web pages. The task itself is foundational to downstream NLP applications including search indexing, retrieval-augmented generation, dataset construction, and LLM training. The paper responds to growing demand for robust evaluation as these applications scale, extending beyond the narrow scope of previous work to encompass diverse page structures and modern web content. It leverages recent advances in LLM-assisted annotation while combining them with traditional quality control pipelines.
enables: WCXB provides infrastructure for developing more robust and generalizable content extraction methods across diverse web page types, rather than optimizing narrowly for news articles. The benchmark enables systematic analysis of which extraction approaches work well for different page structures, facilitating targeted improvements in areas where current systems struggle. This work supports the broader goal of improving information retrieval and LLM training pipelines that depend on clean content extraction at scale. The diverse page type coverage and rigorous annotation methodology establish a foundation for future research on structure-aware extraction, transfer learning across page types, and more sophisticated neural approaches that can adapt to the heterogeneous nature of real-world web content.
